title: The Two Equal Doors
subtitle: A visualisation for picturing receiving and spending as two equally balanced doors, neither larger nor heavier than the other
excerpt: A short visualisation from the Day 79 theme of a 12-week money programme. The image is two equal doors, one for money in and one for money out, both opening smoothly and closing cleanly with no effort on either side.
sourceNotes: Original visualisation created for homemade.education, drawing on the Day 79 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The image of two equal doors is original to this practice.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to reset the feeling of imbalance between how money in and money out feel, and anchor an image of equal ease in both.

content.text: A short visualisation for the Day 79 theme: lock in ease around receiving and spending. The image is two identical doors, balanced exactly, neither heavier than the other.
Sit comfortably. Close your eyes or let your gaze soften.
Picture a corridor. At the far end, two doors stand side by side. They are identical in size, weight, and colour. The one on the left is labelled Receiving. The one on the right is labelled Spending.

Watch the left door open. Money moves through it toward you: a payment, a transfer, an amount arriving in your account. The door opens easily and closes cleanly. No drama on either side of the threshold.
Now watch the right door open. Money moves through it away from you: a bill paid, a purchase made, an amount leaving your account. The door opens with the same ease. The same clean close.
Both doors. Same weight. Same ease. The corridor stays calm while money moves in either direction.
Open your eyes when you are ready. Write one line about what you noticed, if you want.
Original to homemade.education. Draws on the Day 79 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
